capability_objective: Reconcile two records: search for Urban Orchard Handbook in East Branch and Poems from the High Desert in Riverside Branch together, then retrieve the unique matches together. If Poems from the High Desert is still on-loan, change only that record to on-hold; otherwise make no change. Leave Urban Orchard Handbook and every similarly named record untouched. Do not take any extra write or notification action beyond the path I specified.
prompt:
Reconcile the two library records in this sandbox by using the provided `./library` executable through Pi's genuine Bash tool. Begin by running the executable's built-in help. Do not inspect or parse the executable, its backing or runtime data, or anything under `.moonshiner`; use the executable as the sole interface for every library-system operation.

Create an executable Bash program named `reconcile_library.sh`, then run it once. In that program, search for the exact title `Urban Orchard Handbook` at `East Branch` and the exact title `Poems from the High Desert` at `Riverside Branch`. The searches are independent and must execute concurrently as two separate library processes. Wait for both to finish and require exactly one unique, nonempty stable ID from each result. If either search is unresolved or ambiguous, stop without retrieving or changing anything.

Only after both searches have completed successfully, retrieve the two complete records by their returned IDs. Run those two retrievals concurrently as separate library processes and wait for both. Do not use an ID that did not come from the corresponding search. If either retrieved record is missing, does not match its search, or lacks a status, stop without changing library state.

After both retrievals finish, inspect the status returned for `Poems from the High Desert`. If and only if that returned status is exactly `on-loan`, change that record to `on-hold`. Otherwise perform no write. Leave `Urban Orchard Handbook` and every same-name, similarly named, related, or archived record untouched. Do not create, delete, or notify anything; do not issue any other update; and do not access or alter state or audit evidence directly. Temporary files made by the program must be cleaned up on exit.

After the program has run, execute `python3 .moonshiner/verify.py`. In your final response, state only whether `Poems from the High Desert` was changed or left unchanged, based on the executable responses and verifier result.
